proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
Mean pfs_months differs between female (sex_female=1) and male (sex_female=0) patients.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
Worse performance status (higher ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: each additional year of age associates with +0.177 months of PFS (95% CI 0.175 to 0.178). Highly significant.
Mean PFS 4.308 months (female) vs 4.314 months (male). Difference is essentially zero.
Mean PFS 3.703 months (stage IV) vs 5.055 months (non-stage IV); stage IV reduces PFS by ~1.35 months.
Vs ECOG 0: ECOG 1 reduces PFS by 1.218 months (p~0); ECOG 2 reduces PFS by 2.363 months (p~0). Strong monotonic gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Other labs (ast, alt, bilirubin, creatinine, bun, electrolytes) have detectable univariate associations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Each +1 g/dL albumin associates with +0.465 months PFS.
Each +1 ng/mL CEA associates with -0.0053 months PFS.
Each +1 U/L LDH associates with -0.0003 months PFS.
Each +1% weight loss associates with -0.073 months PFS.
Direction negative but small (-0.002 per mg/L), not significant at 0.05.
Univariate slope +0.005, p=0.29 — no significant univariate association.
Univariate slope -0.010, p=0.078 — no significant univariate association in expected direction.
Slope -0.0006 per U/L, p=0.004.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS for ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl
analyses.result_summary: Most of these labs (AST, ALT, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) showed no significant univariate association (all p>0.1). Total bilirubin: slope -0.065, p=0.044.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Right-sided primary tumors (right_sided_primary=1) have shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
nras_mutation=1 is associated with different pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
msi_high=1 is associated with different pfs_months than microsatellite-stable.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with different pfs_months than non-amplified.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with different pfs_months than non-fusion.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.10 (right) vs 4.41 (left); right-sided 0.305 months shorter.
Mean PFS 4.12 (mut) vs 4.45 (WT); KRAS mutation 0.327 months shorter.
Mean PFS 4.53 (NRAS mut) vs 4.31 (WT); NRAS mutation +0.22 months longer (mildly significant).
Mean PFS 4.09 (BRAF V600E) vs 4.32 (WT); BRAF mutation 0.228 months shorter.
Mean PFS 4.29 (MSI-H) vs 4.31 (MSS); essentially no difference.
Mean PFS 4.26 (HER2+) vs 4.31 (HER2-); not significant.
Mean PFS 4.35 (fusion+) vs 4.31 (fusion-); not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Marginally, treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months (positive main effect).
Marginally, treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
Marginally, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
Marginally, treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.
Marginally, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months.
Marginally, treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.28 (treated) vs 4.32 (untreated); -0.038 months, NS.
Mean PFS 4.30 (treated) vs 4.32 (untreated); -0.019 months, NS.
Mean PFS 4.32 (treated) vs 4.31 (untreated); +0.005 months, NS.
Mean PFS 4.32 (treated) vs 4.31 (untreated); +0.005 months, NS.
Mean PFS 4.27 (treated) vs 4.32 (untreated); -0.044 months, NS.
Mean PFS 5.09 (treated) vs 4.12 (untreated); +0.97 months, highly significant. Regorafenib stands alone among the treatments with a strong unadjusted main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutually adjusting for all features, the strongest predictors of pfs_months are ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), age_years (positive), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), kras_mutation (negative), right_sided_primary (negative), braf_v600e (negative), cea_ng_ml (negative), and ldh_u_l (negative). Other lab values have negligible adjusted effects.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.832. Largest adjusted coefficients (signed): ECOG2 -2.34, ECOG1 -1.20, stage_iv -1.36, age_years +0.176/year, albumin +0.464/g/dL, weight_loss -0.076/%, kras_mutation -0.352, right_sided -0.290, braf_v600e -0.350, cea -0.005/ng/mL, ldh -0.0004/U/L. All p<1e-15. Most other labs (AST, ALT, creatinine, BUN, electrolytes, total bili, alk phos) became NS after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for prognostic features, treatment_regorafenib retains a substantial positive main effect on pfs_months, while no other treatment shows a non-zero adjusted main effect.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ all features + all treatments', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefs: cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19), bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73), pembrolizumab +0.0003 (p=0.98), encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.64), trast_tuc +0.026 (p=0.07), regorafenib +0.943 (p~0). Only regorafenib has a meaningful adjusted main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type) patients but not in kras_mutation=1 (negative interaction with KRAS mutation).
treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in braf_v600e=0 patients but not in braf_v600e=1 (negative interaction with BRAF mutation).
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in msi_high=1 patients (positive interaction with MSI-high).
treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=1 patients (positive interaction with BRAF V600E).
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in her2_amplified=1 patients (positive interaction with HER2 amplification).

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab × KRAS interaction: coef -0.022, p=0.19 — not significant. Cetuximab does not show a KRAS-dependent effect in this dataset.
Cetuximab × BRAF V600E interaction: not significant (p=0.78).

Pembrolizumab × MSI-high interaction: not significant (p=0.27). Even within MSI-high (n=2513), pembro vs no-pembro PFS difference is +0.007 months (p=0.96). Pembrolizumab has no detectable effect even in its canonical indication.
Encorafenib × BRAF V600E interaction p=0.85. In BRAF V600E patients (n=2272), encorafenib vs no-encorafenib PFS = 3.98 vs 4.11 (diff -0.13, p=0.33). No detectable effect.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib × HER2 interaction p=0.84. In HER2-amplified (n=1504), tt vs no-tt PFS = 4.27 vs 4.26 (diff +0.02, p=0.93). No detectable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type), with little or no benefit in kras_mutation=1 (negative interaction).
The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in braf_v600e=0 (BRAF wild-type), with little or no benefit in braf_v600e=1 (negative interaction).
The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0), with little or no benefit in right-sided (negative interaction with right-sided).
The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in patients with low cea_ng_ml (negative interaction with CEA above the median).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib × KRAS interaction = -1.632, p~0. Stratified: in KRAS WT (n=29102, treated=5816), regorafenib raises PFS by +1.626 months (p~0); in KRAS mut (n=20898, treated=4206), regorafenib coef = -0.004 (p=0.27). Effect is entirely confined to KRAS-WT.
Adjusted regorafenib × BRAF interaction = -0.991, p=3e-104. Within KRAS-WT subset, the regorafenib × BRAF interaction is -1.770, p=2e-229. BRAF V600E essentially abolishes the regorafenib benefit.
Adjusted regorafenib × right-sided interaction = -1.463, p~0. Within KRAS-WT, the rego × right-sided interaction is -2.524, p~0. Right-sided primary essentially abolishes the regorafenib benefit.
Adjusted regorafenib × (CEA>median) interaction = -1.640, p~0. Within KRAS-WT, this becomes -2.799, p~0. High CEA essentially abolishes the regorafenib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Comprehensive interaction screen: for every non-regorafenib treatment, no biomarker, sidedness, ECOG, age, or lab marker produces a clinically meaningful interaction with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Loop over treatments x [biomarkers, sidedness, age, albumin, ldh, cea, weight_loss, nlr, ECOG], OLS with full feature adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Among ~75 interaction tests for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib against all biomarkers and key labs, only two passed nominal p<0.05 (bev × albumin p=0.014; trast_tuc × ldh p=0.032), neither survives multiple testing or has clinical magnitude. No canonical biomarker-targeted interaction (cetuximab×KRAS/BRAF/sidedness, pembrolizumab×MSI-high, encorafenib×BRAF, trast-tuc×HER2) is significant in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is jointly defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided): in patients meeting all three criteria, regorafenib substantially increases PFS, while in patients failing any criterion, regorafenib has no detectable effect.

analyses.code: Define profile = (kras==0 & braf==0 & right==0); compare regorafenib effect inside vs outside; multivariable model with rego x each of the three modifiers
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=17372 (34.7% of cohort). Inside profile: rego mean=6.875 (n=3461) vs no-rego 4.117 (n=13911); diff=+2.758 months, p~0. Outside profile: rego 4.147 vs no-rego 4.117; diff=+0.030, p=0.29 (essentially zero). Adjusted three-way interaction model: rego main coef in reference cell = +2.290 (p~0), rego×kras = -1.787, rego×braf = -1.770, rego×right_sided = -1.474 (all p~0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml below ~5 ng/mL: in patients meeting all four criteria, regorafenib increases mean PFS by ~5 months; in patients failing any criterion, regorafenib has no clinically meaningful effect.

analyses.code: Search CEA cutoff in {1,2,3,4,4.5,5,7,10,15,20} within KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subset
analyses.result_summary: Cutoff scan within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided stratum: CEA<1 diff=+4.98 (n=1825); CEA<2 +5.04; CEA<3 +5.03; CEA<4 +5.04; CEA<4.5 +5.03; CEA<5 +5.01 (n_below=9326); CEA<7 +4.13; CEA<10 +3.62. The threshold appears to be at CEA~5 ng/mL: below it the effect is fully present and stable, above it the effect collapses to near zero (CEA>=5 diff=+0.005, p=0.99).

analyses.code: Compare regorafenib effect inside the four-way profile vs outside
analyses.result_summary: Full-profile (KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided & CEA<median): n=8640 (17.3% of cohort). Inside: rego mean 9.115 (n=1782) vs no-rego 4.084 (n=6858); diff=+5.030 months, p~0. Outside: rego 4.218 (n=8240) vs no-rego 4.123 (n=33120); diff=+0.095, p=3e-4 (statistically detectable due to large n but clinically negligible).

analyses.code: Within-profile, screen for any further effect modifiers
analyses.result_summary: Within the full profile (n=8640), regorafenib × additional modifiers were all small: sex p=0.98, NRAS p=0.41, MSI p=0.75, HER2 p=0.45, NTRK p=0.87, age>median p=5e-9 but coef +0.05 (clinically negligible), albumin>median p=0.07, LDH>median p=0.73, weight loss p=0.48, CRP p=0.52, NLR p=0.78. ECOG2 vs 0 interaction p=1e-7, coef -0.07 (small reduction). No further modifier substantively changes the +5-month effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the 6 listed treatments, only treatment_regorafenib has a non-trivial effect on pfs_months, and that effect is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0, braf_v600e=0, right_sided_primary=0, and cea_ng_ml<5; cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib show no meaningful PFS benefit in this cohort, even within their canonical biomarker-defined indications.

analyses.code: Stratified canonical-indication tests: cetuximab in KRAS/NRAS/BRAF WT & left; pembrolizumab in MSI-high; encorafenib in BRAF V600E; trast-tuc in HER2-amp
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab in EGFR-like profile (KRAS/NRAS/BRAF WT & left, n=16443): cet vs no-cet diff = -0.067 months, p=0.13. Pembrolizumab in MSI-high (n=2513): diff = +0.007, p=0.96. Encorafenib in BRAF V600E (n=2272): diff = -0.13, p=0.33. Trast-tuc in HER2-amp (n=1504): diff = +0.02, p=0.93. None of the canonical targeted-therapy benefits reproduce in this cohort.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ adj features + rego + rego:kras + rego:braf + rego:right_sided + rego:cea_hi', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted joint interaction model: regorafenib main coef in reference cell (KRAS WT, BRAF WT, left, low CEA) = +3.07 months/yr from this term plus ECOG/age effects giving total ~+5; rego×KRAS = -1.76, rego×BRAF = -1.71, rego×right_sided = -1.46, rego×CEA_hi = -1.60 (all p~0). The four interaction terms together explain why the +5-month effect collapses to ~0 outside the full profile.

analyses.code: Decision-tree T-learner CATE for regorafenib (max_depth=6, min_samples_leaf=200)
analyses.result_summary: Estimated patient-level CATE distribution for regorafenib: mean +0.93 months, sd 1.97, range -3.06 to +7.18. Top CATE quartile vs bottom: KRAS-mut prevalence 8% vs 53%, right-sided 14% vs 44%, CEA median 2.20 vs 5.99 — exactly matches the analytic subgroup, providing independent confirmation.
